prevalence.value: 0.3-0.8% (higher in adolescent girls and young women)
prevalence.source: Eating disorder epidemiology syntheses (Latin America) / WHO context
prevalence.trend: increasing recognition; social media and body image pressures contribute

burden.mortalityRate: Elevated vs general population (medical complications and suicide)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: restriction leading to low weight, intense fear of weight gain, and body image disturbance; medical assessment for malnutrition and complications

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: hundreds of thousands (higher when including subthreshold cases)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; best with multidisciplinary care, which is limited outside major centers

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis often in specialist mental health and pediatric/adolescent services; APS screening limited
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Nutritional rehabilitation + psychotherapy (family-based therapy in adolescents) + medical monitoring; inpatient stabilization for severe malnutrition
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited multidisciplinary eating disorder programs in SUS and delayed presentation
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialized university hospitals can provide multidisciplinary care in large cities
